coverage_explanation: The legal note for CN codes 22041011–22041098 identifies the product as "Sparkling wine" and refers to subheading note 1 of Chapter 22 for the full definition. The existing TBox already contains eucn:SparklingWine as a class with eucn:isCarbonated capturing the carbonation criterion. However, subheading note 1 to Chapter 22 (not reproduced in the supplied legal text) defines sparkling wine more precisely by reference to excess pressure (≥ 3 bar at 20 °C), which is already partially modelled via eucn:ExcessPressureQuality and eucn:excessPressureBar in the running TBox but cannot be attached here without knowing the exact numeric threshold from the note text. Additionally, CN code 22041094 is a specific 8-digit subcode within the 22041011–22041098 range; its distinguishing criteria relative to sibling codes (e.g. by geographical indication, sweetness level, or other quality) are not provided in the supplied legal text, so those cannot be captured. The only axiom that can be unambiguously grounded in the supplied note is the membership in eucn:SparklingWine via the carbonation flag. Coverage is therefore partial.
